fields.name: Admin
fields.description: Full system access — manages users, settings, and all modules.

fields.name: Doctor
fields.description: Clinical access — manages encounters, prescriptions, and patient care.

fields.name: Nurse
fields.description: Records vitals, administers medications, and monitors patients.

fields.name: Receptionist
fields.description: Patient registration, appointment scheduling, and billing intake.

fields.name: Pharmacist
fields.description: Medication dispensing and drug interaction checks.
Furosemide may increase metformin levels. Monitor blood glucose.
PPIs may reduce clopidogrel effectiveness.
Hypokalemia from diuretics increases digoxin toxicity risk.
Increased sedation risk. Avoid alcohol.
Increased GI irritation risk. Avoid concurrent use.
Additive hypotensive effect. Risk of bradycardia and dizziness.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic AV node suppression and vasodilation.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP and HR. Consider dose reduction if symptoms develop.

fields.description: Additive hypotensive effect. Risk of bradycardia.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic AV node suppression and vasodilation.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP and HR. Consider dose reduction if symptoms develop.

fields.description: Dual RAAS blockade increases risk of hypotension, hyperkalemia, and renal impairment.
fields.mechanism: Additive ACE inhibition and AT1 receptor blockade.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Use single RAAS agent. Monitor K+ and creatinine if unavoidable.

fields.description: Dual RAAS blockade increases risk of hypotension, hyperkalemia, and renal impairment.
fields.mechanism: Additive ACE inhibition and AT1 receptor blockade.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Use single RAAS agent. Monitor K+ and creatinine if unavoidable.

fields.description: Dual RAAS blockade — increased risk of hypotension and hyperkalemia.
fields.mechanism: Additive ACE inhibition and AT1 receptor blockade.
fields.recommendation: Avoid. Use single RAAS agent. Monitor K+ if unavoidable.

fields.description: Dual RAAS blockade — increased risk of hypotension and hyperkalemia.
fields.mechanism: Additive ACE inhibition and AT1 receptor blockade.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Monitor renal function and K+.

fields.description: Combined K+-sparing diuretic + ACE inhibitor increases hyperkalemia risk.
fields.mechanism: Aldosterone antagonism plus ACE inhibition reduces K+ excretion.
fields.recommendation: Monitor K+ closely. Limit dietary K+ intake. Consider reducing spironolactone dose.

fields.description: ARB + K+-sparing diuretic increases hyperkalemia risk.
fields.mechanism: Aldosterone antagonism plus AT1 blockade reduces K+ excretion.
fields.recommendation: Monitor K+ regularly. Avoid if baseline K+ >5.0.

fields.description: ARB + K+-sparing diuretic increases hyperkalemia risk.
fields.mechanism: Aldosterone antagonism plus AT1 blockade reduces K+ excretion.
fields.recommendation: Monitor K+ regularly. Avoid if baseline K+ >5.0.

fields.description: Thiazide-induced hypokalemia increases digoxin toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: HCTZ depletes K+, potentiating digoxin arrhythmogenicity.
fields.recommendation: Monitor K+. Maintain K+ >4.0. Consider K+ supplementation.

fields.description: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70-100%. Risk of bradycardia and toxicity.
fields.mechanism: Amiodarone reduces renal digoxin clearance and displaces tissue binding.
fields.recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% on starting amiodarone. Monitor levels and ECG.

fields.description: Omeprazole reduces clopidogrel activation via CYP2C19, potentially reducing antiplatelet effect.
fields.mechanism: CYP2C19 inhibition by omeprazole reduces conversion to active metabolite.
fields.recommendation: Use pantoprazole or rabeprazole instead (less CYP2C19 interaction).

fields.description: Minimal interaction. Pantoprazole has weaker CYP2C19 inhibition.
fields.mechanism: Pantoprazole undergoes non-enzymatic metabolism, minimal CYP interaction.
fields.recommendation: Safe for concurrent use. No dose adjustment needed.

fields.description: Increased risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis with statin-fibrate combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive HMG-CoA reductase inhibition and PPAR-alpha activation.
fields.recommendation: Monitor CK levels. Advise patient to report muscle pain. Avoid in renal impairment.

fields.description: Increased risk of myopathy with statin-fibrate combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive effect on skeletal muscle.
fields.recommendation: Monitor CK. Use lowest effective statin dose. Avoid in renal impairment.

fields.description: Ibuprofen may reduce aspirin's antiplatelet effect and increase GI bleeding risk.
fields.mechanism: Competitive inhibition of COX-1 acetylation; additive GI mucosal damage.
fields.recommendation: Take ibuprofen at least 30 min after aspirin. Consider gastroprotection.

fields.description: Increased GI bleeding risk with NSAID + aspirin combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive COX-1 inhibition leading to gastric mucosal damage.
fields.recommendation: Add PPI for gastroprotection. Use lowest NSAID dose, shortest duration.

fields.description: Increased GI bleeding risk. Additive COX-1 inhibition.
fields.mechanism: Additive COX-1 inhibition leading to gastric mucosal injury.
fields.recommendation: Consider gastroprotection with PPI. Monitor for GI symptoms.

fields.description: Significantly increased bleeding risk. Avoid combination if possible.
fields.mechanism: Additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects.
fields.recommendation: Avoid. If unavoidable, monitor INR closely. Use lowest effective doses.

fields.description: Metronidazole potentiates anticoagulant effect — INR may double.
fields.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits hepatic metabolism of oral anticoagulants.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR every 2-3 days during and for 1 week after metronidazole course.

fields.description: Fluoroquinolones potentiate anticoagulant effect. Increased INR.
fields.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, reducing anticoagulant metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR closely (every 2-3 days). Adjust dose as needed.

fields.description: Rifampicin dramatically reduces anticoagulant effect via enzyme induction.
fields.mechanism: Rifampicin induces CYP2C9, increasing anticoagulant metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR frequently. May need 2-3x dose increase. Warn patient.

fields.description: Contraindicated. Phenylbutazone potentiates anticoagulant effect with severe bleeding risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP inhibition. Displaces protein binding.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT USE together. Choose alternative NSAID.

fields.description: Additive hypoglycemic effect. Monitor blood glucose closely.
fields.mechanism: Combined biguanide + sulfonylurea increases insulin secretion and sensitivity.
fields.recommendation: Monitor fasting and post-prandial glucose. Educate patient on hypoglycemia symptoms.

fields.description: Additive hypoglycemic effect. Increased risk of hypoglycemia.
fields.mechanism: Combined biguanide + sulfonylurea action.
fields.recommendation: Glucose monitoring. Adjust sulfonylurea dose if needed.

fields.description: Additive glucose-lowering effect. Adjust insulin dose to avoid hypoglycemia.
fields.mechanism: Combined insulin sensitizer plus exogenous insulin.
fields.recommendation: Monitor glucose. Start insulin at low dose and titrate.

fields.description: Additive glucose lowering. Usually a well-tolerated combination.
fields.mechanism: Dual insulin sensitizer effect (AMPK + PPAR-γ).
fields.recommendation: Monitor HbA1c. Watch for fluid retention with pioglitazone.

fields.description: Beta-blockers mask hypoglycemia symptoms (tachycardia, palpitations). Increased hypoglycemia risk.
fields.mechanism: Beta-blockade blunts adrenergic response to hypoglycemia.
fields.recommendation: Warn patient about masked hypoglycemia. Monitor glucose more frequently.

fields.description: Fluconazole increases glimepiride levels — severe hypoglycemia risk.
fields.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 metabolism of glimepiride.
fields.recommendation: Monitor glucose closely. Consider reducing glimepiride dose by 50%.

fields.description: Clarithromycin increases sulfonylurea levels — hypoglycemia risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition reduces glimepiride metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor glucose. Consider temporary dose reduction.

fields.description: Additive volume depletion. Risk of hypotension and dehydration.
fields.mechanism: Combined SGLT2 inhibition + loop diuretic enhances fluid loss.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP and renal function. Ensure adequate hydration.

fields.description: Additive volume depletion risk. Combined diuretic effect.
fields.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibition + loop diuretic — synergistic fluid loss.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP, electrolytes, and renal function.

fields.description: Additive glucose lowering. SGLT2 inhibitors reduce insulin dose requirement.
fields.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibition reduces renal glucose reabsorption, independent of insulin.
fields.recommendation: Reduce insulin dose by 10-20% when starting SGLT2 inhibitor. Monitor for DKA.

fields.description: Additive glucose lowering. Monitor for fluid retention.
fields.mechanism: PPAR-γ agonist plus exogenous insulin.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for edema and weight gain. Reduce insulin dose if needed.

fields.description: Additive glucose lowering. Well tolerated combination.
fields.mechanism: Alpha-glucosidase inhibition + insulin sensitizer.
fields.recommendation: Monitor post-prandial glucose. Treat hypoglycemia with glucose (not sucrose).

fields.description: Rifampicin reduces contraceptive efficacy — risk of pregnancy.
fields.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 induction increases estrogen/progestin metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Use alternative non-hormonal contraception during and for 4 weeks after rifampicin.

fields.description: Rifampicin may reduce metformin efficacy.
fields.mechanism: CYP induction and possible OATP inhibition affect metformin levels.
fields.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose. Adjust metformin dose as needed.

fields.description: Rifampicin may reduce dapagliflozin efficacy via UGT induction.
fields.mechanism: UGT1A9 and UGT2B4 induction by rifampicin increases dapagliflozin metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor glucose. Consider dose adjustment.

fields.description: Combined hepatotoxicity risk. Rifampicin increases INH metabolite toxicity.
fields.mechanism: Rifampicin induces hydrazine formation from INH, increasing liver injury risk.
fields.recommendation: Monitor LFTs monthly. Warn patient about symptoms of hepatitis.

fields.description: INH increases carbamazepine levels — toxicity risk (ataxia, dizziness, sedation).
fields.mechanism: INH inhibits CYP3A4, reducing carbamazepine metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor carbamazepine levels. Reduce dose by 20-30% if needed.

fields.description: INH increases phenytoin levels — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: INH inhibits CYP2C9/2C19, reducing phenytoin metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels. Reduce phenytoin dose by 30%.

fields.description: Disulfiram-like reaction: severe nausea, vomiting, flushing, headache.
fields.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, causing acetaldehyde accumulation.
fields.recommendation: Advise complete avoidance of alcohol during treatment and for 48h after last dose.

fields.description: Increased CNS stimulation risk (seizures, dizziness). Avoid in epilepsy.
fields.mechanism: GABA-A receptor antagonism is potentiated by NSAIDs.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination in patients with seizure history. Use caution.

fields.description: Ciprofloxacin increases theophylline levels — toxicity risk (seizures, arrhythmias).
fields.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, reducing theophylline clearance by 30-50%.
fields.recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by 50%. Monitor theophylline levels.

fields.description: Levofloxacin may increase theophylline levels. Less interaction than ciprofloxacin.
fields.mechanism: Weak CYP1A2 inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Monitor theophylline levels. Dose adjustment may not be needed.

fields.description: Azithromycin may increase digoxin levels via P-gp inhibition.
fields.mechanism: P-glycoprotein inhibition in gut reduces digoxin efflux, increasing absorption.
fields.recommendation: Monitor digoxin levels. Watch for signs of digoxin toxicity.

fields.description: Azithromycin may increase statin levels — myopathy risk.
fields.mechanism: Macrolide inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism of statins.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for muscle pain. Consider temporary statin dose reduction.

fields.description: Clarithromycin significantly increases atorvastatin levels — rhabdomyolysis risk.
fields.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin.
fields.recommendation: Use azithromycin instead. If unavoidable, hold statin during clarithromycin course.

fields.description: Fluconazole increases statin levels — myopathy risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP3A4 and CYP2C9 inhibition by fluconazole.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for muscle pain. Consider reducing statin dose.

fields.description: Fluconazole reduces losartan active metabolite formation — reduced efficacy.
fields.mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition blocks conversion to active E-3174 metabolite.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP. Consider alternative ARB or increase losartan dose.

fields.description: May reduce artesunate efficacy. Increased ECG monitoring recommended.
fields.mechanism: Possible pharmacodynamic antagonism. QT prolongation additive.
fields.recommendation: Monitor ECG. Use alternative partner drug if possible.

fields.description: Additive QT prolongation — risk of torsades de pointes.
fields.mechanism: Both drugs prolong ventricular repolarization.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, monitor ECG and K+/Mg+.

fields.description: Additive nephrotoxicity. Both drugs are renally excreted.
fields.mechanism: Competition for renal tubular secretion.
fields.recommendation: Monitor renal function. Ensure adequate hydration.

fields.description: Increased GI bleeding risk. Additive COX inhibition.
fields.mechanism: Additive COX-1 inhibition leads to gastric mucosal damage.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Add PPI if needed. Use single NSAID.

fields.description: No added benefit, increased GI and renal toxicity.
fields.mechanism: Additive COX inhibition with no additional analgesia.
fields.recommendation: Do NOT combine. Choose one NSAID at appropriate dose.

fields.description: Generally safe combination. Alternate for better pain control.
fields.mechanism: Different analgesic mechanisms — COX inhibition + central COX-3.
fields.recommendation: Commonly used combination in Indian practice. Safe at recommended doses.

fields.description: Common combination in Indian practice. Generally safe short-term.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic analgesia via COX and central pathways.
fields.recommendation: Safe for short-term use. Avoid in active PUD.

fields.description: NSAIDs reduce antihypertensive efficacy of ACE inhibitors. Risk of renal impairment.
fields.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandin synthesis needed for ACE inhibitor action.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP and renal function. Consider alternative analgesia.

fields.description: NSAIDs reduce ARB antihypertensive effect. Risk of acute kidney injury.
fields.mechanism: Prostaglandin inhibition reduces vasodilatory effect of ARBs.
fields.recommendation: Monitor BP. Use lowest NSAID dose. Stay hydrated.

fields.description: NSAIDs reduce diuretic efficacy and increase nephrotoxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: COX inhibition reduces prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow.
fields.recommendation: Monitor renal function and urine output. Consider alternative analgesia.

fields.description: Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures. Both lower seizure threshold.
fields.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effect. Both inhibit serotonin reuptake.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Use alternative analgesic if patient is on TCAs.

fields.description: Serotonin syndrome risk: agitation, hyperthermia, clonus, hyperreflexia.
fields.mechanism: Additive serotonin reuptake inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Avoid. Use non-opioid analgesic. If unavoidable, start at lowest dose and monitor.

fields.description: Serotonin syndrome risk with SSRI + tramadol combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive serotonin reuptake inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Avoid combination. Choose alternative analgesic.

fields.description: Increased serotonin syndrome risk. Ondansetron is a 5-HT3 antagonist.
fields.mechanism: Complex serotonergic interaction — ondansetron may reduce tramadol efficacy.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for serotonin toxicity. Consider alternative antiemetic.

fields.description: Additive sedation and dizziness. Common neuropathic pain combination.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic CNS depression.
fields.recommendation: Start at low doses. Warn about driving/machinery. Common but safe if titrated.

fields.description: Additive CNS depression — excessive sedation, reduced coordination.
fields.mechanism: GABAergic potentiation plus calcium channel modulation.
fields.recommendation: Reduce doses of both. Warn about driving and falls.

fields.description: Additive respiratory depression and sedation risk.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic CNS depression via GABA and opioid receptors.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for excessive sedation. Reduce doses in elderly.

fields.description: PPIs may reduce methotrexate clearance — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: Competitive inhibition of renal tubular secretion.
fields.recommendation: Withhold PPI during high-dose methotrexate. Monitor levels.

fields.description: Contraindicated: additive QT prolongation, risk of torsades de pointes.
fields.mechanism: Both drugs block hERG K+ channels, prolonging QT interval.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT COMBINE. Use alternative antiemetic (ondansetron).

fields.description: Additive QT prolongation. Risk of cardiac arrhythmias.
fields.mechanism: Combined hERG channel blockade.
fields.recommendation: Monitor ECG. Use alternative antiemetic or antibiotic if possible.

fields.description: Both drugs prolong QT interval. Moderate risk.
fields.mechanism: hERG K+ channel blockade additive effect.
fields.recommendation: Use in caution. Monitor ECG in elderly or those with cardiac disease.

fields.description: Sucralfate reduces fluoroquinolone absorption by >50%.
fields.mechanism: Cationic binding — sucralfate contains aluminum ions that chelate quinolones.
fields.recommendation: Space doses: ciprofloxacin 2h before or 6h after sucralfate.

fields.description: Sucralfate reduces thyroxine absorption — may worsen hypothyroidism.
fields.mechanism: Physical binding and reduced GI absorption.
fields.recommendation: Space by at least 4 hours. Monitor TSH in 6-8 weeks.

fields.description: Mg/Al-containing antacids reduce fluoroquinolone absorption by 50-90%.
fields.mechanism: Cation chelation of quinolone ring.
fields.recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2h before or 4h after antacid.

fields.description: Ondansetron may reduce tramadol's analgesic efficacy. Serotonin syndrome risk.
fields.mechanism: 5-HT3 antagonism may block tramadol's serotonergic analgesia.
fields.recommendation: Monitor pain control. Consider alternative antiemetic.

fields.description: Ciprofloxacin increases theophylline levels by 30-50% — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP1A2 inhibition reduces theophylline clearance.
fields.recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by 50%. Monitor levels. Consider alternative antibiotic.

fields.description: Ciprofloxacin increases deriphylline levels — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP1A2 inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Reduce dose. Monitor for nausea, palpitations, seizures.

fields.description: Beta-blockers reduce theophylline clearance and oppose bronchodilation.
fields.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic antagonism on bronchial smooth muscle.
fields.recommendation: Use cardioselective beta-blocker if needed. Monitor theophylline levels.

fields.description: Aspirin may exacerbate asthma in sensitive patients. No direct interaction.
fields.mechanism: Aspirin-sensitive asthma — leukotriene pathway.
fields.recommendation: Warn about aspirin use in asthma. Montelukast may partially protect.

fields.description: Contraindicated: serotonin syndrome risk with MAO inhibitor activity.
fields.mechanism: Linezolid has MAOI activity. Combined with SSRI causes serotonin excess.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT COMBINE. Choose alternative antibiotic.

fields.description: Contraindicated: serotonin syndrome risk with MAO inhibitor activity.
fields.mechanism: Linezolid MAOI + SSRI = serotonin excess.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT COMBINE. Choose alternative antibiotic.

fields.description: Contraindicated: serotonin syndrome risk.
fields.mechanism: MAO inhibitor activity of linezolid plus SSRI.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT COMBINE. Use alternative antibiotic.

fields.description: Increased bleeding risk. SSRIs impair platelet function; NSAIDs add GI bleeding.
fields.mechanism: SSRI depletes platelet serotonin. NSAIDs inhibit COX-1.
fields.recommendation: Add PPI for gastroprotection. Monitor for bruising/bleeding.

fields.description: Increased bleeding risk. SSRI + NSAID combination.
fields.mechanism: Impaired platelet aggregation plus GI mucosal injury.
fields.recommendation: Consider gastroprotection. Monitor for GI bleeding.

fields.description: Increased bleeding risk. Additive effect on platelet function.
fields.mechanism: SSRI depletes platelet serotonin + aspirin irreversibly inhibits COX-1.
fields.recommendation: Consider PPI. Monitor for signs of bleeding.

fields.description: Metronidazole increases phenytoin levels — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP2C9/2C19 inhibition by metronidazole.
fields.recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels. Reduce dose if needed.

fields.description: Fluconazole significantly increases phenytoin levels — toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition reduces phenytoin clearance.
fields.recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels. May need 30-50% dose reduction.

fields.description: Aspirin displaces valproate from protein binding — increased free fraction, toxicity risk.
fields.mechanism: Protein binding displacement and beta-oxidation inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Monitor valproate levels. Watch for sedation, tremor, hepatotoxicity.

fields.description: Additive CNS depression. Both potentiate GABA.
fields.mechanism: Synergistic GABAergic effect.
fields.recommendation: Monitor sedation. Use lowest effective doses.

fields.description: Carbamazepine reduces contraceptive efficacy via CYP3A4 induction.
fields.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 induction increases estrogen/progestin clearance.
fields.recommendation: Use alternative contraception (higher-dose pill or non-hormonal).

fields.description: Carbamazepine reduces statin levels — reduced efficacy.
fields.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction increases statin metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor lipids. Consider increasing statin dose or using rosuvastatin.

fields.description: Ciprofloxacin increases olanzapine levels — sedation and metabolic effects.
fields.mechanism: CYP1A2 inhibition reduces olanzapine clearance.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for increased sedation. Consider reducing olanzapine dose.

fields.description: Fluoxetine may increase olanzapine levels. Usually well tolerated.
fields.mechanism: CYP2D6 and CYP1A2 interactions.
fields.recommendation: Monitor weight and metabolic parameters. Common combination.

fields.description: PPIs reduce thyroxine absorption — may worsen hypothyroidism.
fields.mechanism: Gastric pH elevation reduces thyroxine dissolution and absorption.
fields.recommendation: Take thyroxine on empty stomach, 30 min before PPI. Monitor TSH.

fields.description: Calcium supplements reduce thyroxine absorption by 20-25%.
fields.mechanism: Chelation of thyroxine molecules with calcium ions.
fields.recommendation: Space by at least 4 hours. Common in postmenopausal women on both.

fields.description: Iron supplements reduce thyroxine absorption significantly.
fields.mechanism: Physical binding between iron and thyroxine in GI tract.
fields.recommendation: Space by at least 4 hours. Common in anemia + hypothyroidism patients.

fields.description: Antithyroid drugs reduce anticoagulant effect when hyperthyroid state corrects.
fields.mechanism: Metabolic changes as thyroid normalizes affect clotting factor synthesis.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR frequently when starting carbimazole in warfarin patients.

fields.description: PTU potentiates warfarin effect — increased INR and bleeding risk.
fields.mechanism: Reduced vitamin K-dependent clotting factor metabolism.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR. Reduce warfarin dose as thyroid function normalizes.

fields.description: Contraindicated: triple therapy bleeding risk unless essential (e.g., recent stent).
fields.mechanism: Dual anticoagulation + antiplatelet = multiplicative bleeding risk.
fields.recommendation: Avoid unless indicated for mechanical valve + recent stent. Monitor INR closely.

fields.description: NSAIDs with anticoagulant — significantly increased GI and systemic bleeding.
fields.mechanism: COX-1 inhibition plus anticoagulation.
fields.recommendation: Use paracetamol for analgesia. Add PPI if NSAID unavoidable.

fields.description: Broad-spectrum antibiotics can potentiate anticoagulant effect.
fields.mechanism: Gut flora reduction decreases vitamin K synthesis.
fields.recommendation: Monitor INR during and 1-2 weeks after antibiotic course.

fields.description: Increased bleeding risk with LMWH + antiplatelet combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for bleeding. Use lowest effective doses. Common in ACS protocols.

fields.description: Increased thrombotic risk with tranexamic acid + estrogen combination.
fields.mechanism: Additive prothrombotic effect.
fields.recommendation: Avoid in women on OCPs. Use alternative haemostatic agent.

fields.description: Theoretical opposing effects. Tranexamic acid may reduce anticoagulant effect.
fields.mechanism: Antifibrinolytic agent opposes LMWH's anticoagulant action.
fields.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for thrombotic/thrombolytic balance.

fields.description: Mild additive sedation. Less sedating than cetirizine.
fields.mechanism: CNS depression additive effect.
fields.recommendation: Advise caution with alcohol. Less sedating than first-gen antihistamines.

fields.description: Additive anticholinergic effects — dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention, confusion.
fields.mechanism: Combined muscarinic receptor blockade.
fields.recommendation: Avoid in elderly. Monitor for anticholinergic toxicity.

fields.description: Serotonin syndrome risk. Dextromethorphan has serotonergic activity.
fields.mechanism: Additive serotonin reuptake inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for serotonin syndrome. Use alternative antitussive.

fields.description: Cough suppression counteracts mucolytic expectorant effect minimally.
fields.mechanism: Codeine suppresses cough reflex, reducing clearance of thinned secretions.
fields.recommendation: Generally safe. Ensure adequate hydration.

fields.description: Life-threatening hyperkalemia risk with potassium supplementation + K-sparing diuretic.
fields.mechanism: Combined K+ loading with reduced K+ excretion.
fields.recommendation: DO NOT SUPPLEMENT K+ with K-sparing diuretics. Monitor K+ regularly.

fields.description: ACE inhibitors reduce K+ excretion — risk of hyperkalemia with supplements.
fields.mechanism: ACE inhibition reduces aldosterone, decreasing K+ excretion.
fields.recommendation: Avoid K+ supplements with ACE inhibitors unless deficiency confirmed.

fields.description: Folic acid supplementation may reduce methotrexate efficacy in RA.
fields.mechanism: Dihydrofolate reductase inhibition bypassed by exogenous folate.
fields.recommendation: Timing: give folic acid on days without methotrexate. Continue for side effect reduction.

fields.description: Vitamin D-induced hypercalcemia potentiates digoxin toxicity (arrhythmias, heart block).
fields.mechanism: Hypercalcemia increases myocardial sensitivity to digoxin.
fields.recommendation: Monitor serum calcium. Avoid high-dose vitamin D in patients on digoxin.

fields.description: Thiazide diuretics reduce urinary calcium excretion — mild hypercalcemia risk with high-dose vitamin D.
fields.mechanism: HCTZ decreases Ca++ excretion, vitamin D increases absorption.
fields.recommendation: Monitor serum calcium with high-dose vitamin D + thiazide.

fields.description: Increased GI bleeding risk. Corticosteroid + NSAID combination.
fields.mechanism: Corticosteroids impair mucosal defense; aspirin adds COX inhibition.
fields.recommendation: Add PPI. Use lowest steroid dose and shortest duration.

fields.description: Steroids cause hyperglycemia — significantly increased insulin requirement.
fields.mechanism: Corticosteroid-induced insulin resistance.
fields.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose 4-6 hourly. Titrate insulin aggressively.

fields.description: Steroid-induced hyperglycemia may require increased OAD dose.
fields.mechanism: Corticosteroid-induced insulin resistance.
fields.recommendation: Monitor glucose. May need temporary insulin.

fields.description: Increased bleeding risk. Common in COVID-19 thromboprophylaxis.
fields.mechanism: Dual anticoagulant + antiplatelet effect.
fields.recommendation: Monitor for bleeding. Use PPI. Common in high-risk COVID-19 protocols.

fields.transcript: Hello doctor, I have a slight chest pain and headache and my nostrils are closed.
fields.note_text:
## Chief Complaints
Chest pain, headache, nasal congestion.

## History of Present Illness
Patient reports onset and duration of symptoms are not specified. Chest pain described as mild. Aggravating/relieving factors not mentioned.

## Cardiovascular Risk Factors
Not specified / Not documented.

## Vitals
Not specified / Not documented.

## Physical Exam
Not specified / Not documented.

## Investigations
Not specified / Not documented.

## Diagnosis
Differential: Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS), Non-Cardiac Chest Pain, Sinusitis. Diagnosis pending further workup.

## Treatment Plan
No treatment initiated. Further evaluation and investigations required before management.

fields.transcript: ഡോക്ടറെ എനിക്കാണെങ്കിൽ ഭയങ്കര തലവേദന, പനി, ചുമ, ദേഹവേദന, ഭയങ്കര മൂക്കൊലിപ്പ് ഇതൊക്കെയുണ്ട്. രാത്രി ഉറങ്ങാനൊന്നും പറ്റുന്നില്ല.
fields.note_text:
**Chief Complaints**
- Severe headache
- Fever
- Cough
- Myalgia (body ache)
- Rhinorrhea (runny nose)

**History of Present Illness**
- Patient presents with acute onset of multiple systemic symptoms.
- Reports significant insomnia, preventing sleep at night.
- Aggravating/relieving factors and precise duration of symptoms are not specified.

**Cardiovascular Risk Factors**
- None mentioned in the provided dictation.

**Vitals**
- Not mentioned in the provided dictation.

**Physical Exam**
- No physical examination findings provided in the dictation.

**Investigations**
- No investigations mentioned in the provided dictation.

**Diagnosis**
- Differential Diagnosis:
    1. Viral Syndrome (e.g., Influenza)
    2. Acute Respiratory Infection
- No primary cardiac pathology suggested by the provided history.

**Treatment Plan**
- No specific treatment plan mentioned in the provided dictation. Further evaluation would be required to formulate a treatment plan.

fields.transcript: ഡോക്ടറെ എനിക്കാണെങ്കിൽ ഭയങ്കര തലവേദന, ശരീരവേദന, മൂക്കൊലിപ്പ്, പിന്നെ രാത്രി ഉറങ്ങാൻ പറ്റുന്നില്ല.
